perturbation.original_text: III. Exceptions . The confidentiality obligations hereunder shall not apply to Confidential Information which: A. is, or later becomes, public knowledge other than by breach of the provisions of this Agreement; or B. is in the possession of the Party with the full right to disclose prior to its receipt from the Disclosing Party, as evidenced by written records; or C. is independently received by the Receiving Party from a third party, with no restrictions on disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: III. Exceptions . The confidentiality obligations hereunder shall not apply to Confidential Information which: A. is, or later becomes, public knowledge; or B. is in the possession of the Party with the full right to disclose prior to its receipt from the Disclosing Party, as evidenced by written records; or C. is independently received by the Receiving Party from a third party, with no restrictions on disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'other than by breach of the provisions of this Agreement' from Section III.A, we now have a contradiction. If information becomes public knowledge due to a breach of the agreement by the Receiving Party, it is now still considered an exception to confidentiality obligations. This directly contradicts the initial spirit and intent of the Non-Disclosure Agreement, which seeks to protect against unauthorized disclosure. The very definition of exception clause is modified and creates a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: III. Exceptions . The confidentiality obligations hereunder shall not apply to Confidential Information which: A. is, or later becomes, public knowledge; or B. is in the possession of the Party with the full right to disclose prior to its receipt from the Disclosing Party, as evidenced by written records; or C. is independently received by the Receiving Party from a third party, with no restrictions on disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section III.A removes the clause 'other than by breach of the provisions of this Agreement'. This creates a direct contradiction because it now implies that even if confidential information becomes public knowledge *due to a breach of the agreement by the Receiving Party*, it is still considered an exception to the confidentiality obligations. This conflicts with the fundamental intent of a Non-Disclosure Agreement, which is to prevent unauthorized disclosures. As such, the changed text contradicts the contradicted text, as explained in the 'Perturbation Explanation'.

perturbation.original_text: XII. No Solicitation of Employees . The Receiving Party agrees that it will not, for a period of five (5) years from the date of this Agreement, initiate contact with the other Party's employees in order to solicit, entice or induce any employee of the other Party to terminate an employment relationship with the other Party to accept employment with the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: XII. No Solicitation of Employees . The Receiving Party agrees that it will, for a period of five (5) years from the date of this Agreement, initiate contact with the other Party's employees in order to solicit, entice or induce any employee of the other Party to terminate an employment relationship with the other Party to accept employment with the Receiving Party, provided, however, that this restriction does not apply to employees who have already expressed interest in working for the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'provided, however, that this restriction does not apply to employees who have already expressed interest in working for the Receiving Party,' we introduce a contradiction. The initial part of the clause prohibits solicitation, but the added condition allows solicitation of specific employees. This creates ambiguity about whether any solicitation is actually prohibited, undermining the purpose of the clause. It is contradicting with the beginning of the clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: XII. No Solicitation of Employees . The Receiving Party agrees that it will not, for a period of five (5) years from the date of this Agreement, initiate contact with the other Party's employees in order to solicit, entice or induce any employee of the other Party to terminate an employment relationship with the other Party to accept employment with the Receiving Party, provided, however, that this restriction does not apply to employees who have already expressed interest in working for the Receiving Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by initially prohibiting solicitation of employees but then creating an exception for employees already interested in working for the Receiving Party. This directly contradicts the initial prohibition and introduces ambiguity, making the clause self-contradictory, which aligns with the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: XIII. Term and Termination . This Agreement shall commence on the date first written above. The Receiving Party's right to use the Confidential Information in connection with the Business Purposes shall continue in effect until the period of one year from the date above or the other Party provides the Receiving Party with written notice of termination of such right, whichever is earlier. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party's obligations with respect to the Confidential Information hereunder shall continue in full force for at least five years from the termination date and/or effect until further notice from the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: XIII. Term and Termination . This Agreement shall commence on the date first written above. The Receiving Party's right to use the Confidential Information in connection with the Business Purposes shall continue in effect until the period of one year from the date above or the other Party provides the Receiving Party with written notice of termination of such right, whichever is earlier. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party's obligations with respect to the Confidential Information hereunder shall continue in full force until further notice from the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original Section XIII states that confidentiality obligations last 'for at least five years from the termination date and/or effect until further notice.' The modified version removes 'at least five years from the termination date and/or effect,' resulting in confidentiality obligations lasting only 'until further notice.' This creates a direct contradiction. The original text provides a minimum duration (five years), while the modified text implies that the confidentiality obligations can be terminated immediately with notice, even if it is before the five years. This adds uncertainty to the longevity of the confidentiality terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: XIII. Term and Termination . This Agreement shall commence on the date first written above. The Receiving Party's right to use the Confidential Information in connection with the Business Purposes shall continue in effect until the period of one year from the date above or the other Party provides the Receiving Party with written notice of termination of such right, whichever is earlier. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Receiving Party's obligations with respect to the Confidential Information hereunder shall continue in full force until further notice from the other Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a minimum confidentiality period of five years after termination, while the changed text removes this minimum duration, making the confidentiality obligations last only until further notice. This contradicts the original requirement of a minimum five-year confidentiality period. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text because the changed text modifies the same section that defines the duration of confidentiality. According to the provided explanation, this creates a direct contradiction and uncertainty, hence it is a "YES".

perturbation.original_text: XVIII. Governing Law . This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India and the Party’s country laws on equal force. If there is any conflict of laws, the law of India shall prevail.
perturbation.changed_text: XVIII. Governing Law . This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India and the Party’s country laws on equal force. If there is any conflict of laws, the law of The Party’s country shall prevail.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that 'If there is any conflict of laws, the law of India shall prevail.' The modified version changes this to 'If there is any conflict of laws, the law of The Party’s country shall prevail.' This directly contradicts the first sentence of Section XVIII, creating confusion and uncertainty about which jurisdiction's laws will govern the agreement in case of a legal dispute. There are contradictions within the whole section itself now. The initial preference is for Indian laws, but if there is a conflict, then it goes the other way round.
perturbation.contradicted_text: XVIII. Governing Law . This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India and the Party’s country laws on equal force. If there is any conflict of laws, the law of The Party’s country shall prevail.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the governing law clause, stating that the party's country law prevails in case of conflict, directly contradicting the contradicted text, which states the exact same thing. There is a clear and direct conflict within the document regarding which law takes precedence, creating a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, as a clause is placed in multiple locations (within the same section) with conflicting implications. The initial preference is for Indian laws, but if there is a conflict, then it goes the other way round, back to the same clause within the same section. The explanation provided is also correct.
